domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware
source.raw_idea_title: Split Pump Bearing Housing With Sight Glass Bosses

requirements.part_name: Split Pump Bearing Housing With Sight Glass Bosses - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Split Pump Bearing Housing With Sight Glass
Bosses'. The broader use case is: Serviceable bearing support for horizontal industrial pump shafts
in process plants. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Two-piece cast bearing
housing body All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Supports rotating shaft bearings while providing inspection,
lubrication access, and removable maintenance covers. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Combines split-line geometry, bearing seats, cover flanges, drain bosses,
inspection windows, and asymmetric service features that stress feature hierarchy without relying on
simple prismatic forms. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 90 mm x 85 mm x 42 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 10 mm on the length centerline at X=17 mm and X=73 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 30 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=45 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 28 mm x 19 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Split Pump Bearing Housing With Sight Glass Bosses'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
requirements.immutable_requirement_note: After review, this English requirement is the product truth for later SubCAD code generation. Do not simplify it to fit current SubCAD limitations.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware

requirements.part_name: Cartridge Mechanical Seal Gland Assembly - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Cartridge Mechanical Seal Gland Assembly'. The
broader use case is: Preassembled seal module for centrifugal pump shaft sealing during plant
maintenance. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Ported seal gland plate All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Holds seal faces, flush ports, sleeve interface, and gland bolting in a
replaceable cartridge package. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Requires coaxial shaft openings, distributed fastener pads, radial port bosses, stepped faces, and
nested ring-like regions that challenge annular part modeling. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank.
Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same
piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 150 mm x 120 mm x 3 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 11 mm on the length centerline at X=24 mm and X=126 mm, Y=60 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 50 mm long x 11 mm wide through the part, centered at X=75 mm, Y=60 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 37 mm x 40 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 5 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 3 mm deep.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

source.raw_idea_title: Flexible Spacer Coupling Guard With Inspection Windows

requirements.part_name: Flexible Spacer Coupling Guard With Inspection Windows - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Flexible Spacer Coupling Guard With Inspection
Windows'. The broader use case is: Safety enclosure around spacer couplings between motors and
rotating pumps. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Curved perforated coupling
guard shell All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Prevents contact with rotating coupling components while
allowing visual checks and partial access for service. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests curved sheet-like shells, removable end bands, repeated ventilation
openings, cutout windows, and bracket interfaces in a single protective part. The part is made from
low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or
plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into
that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware

requirements.part_name: Pump Impeller Puller Yoke - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pump Impeller Puller Yoke'. The broader use case
is: Workshop tool used to remove stuck impellers from pump shafts without damaging the casing
interface. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Forked puller yoke All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Applies axial extraction force through a central forcing screw while gripping
behind the impeller hub. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Introduces
forked load paths, thick ribs, threaded center boss representation, relief clearances, and ergonomic
wrench flats that push beyond ordinary bracket geometry. The part is made from low-carbon steel,
ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 195 mm x 50 mm x 45 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=185 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 65 mm long x 11 mm wide through the part, centered at X=97 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 48 mm x 18 mm x 16 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M6 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

requirements.part_name: Bearing Heater Support Cradle - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bearing Heater Support Cradle'. The broader use
case is: Maintenance fixture for safely holding pump bearings during controlled installation
heating. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Adjustable V-cradle support frame
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Supports circular bearings above a heater base while accommodating
multiple bearing outside profiles. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Includes angled contact faces, sliding supports, locking features, heat-clearance openings, and
repeated but nonidentical cradle elements that test parametric intent. The part is made from low-
carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal
block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled
faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=165 mm, Y=22 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 58 mm long x 17 mm wide through the part, centered at X=87 mm, Y=22 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 43 mm x 18 mm x 3 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 44 degrees over the last 35 mm of length.

source.raw_idea_title: Stuffing Box Lantern Ring Extraction Tool

requirements.part_name: Stuffing Box Lantern Ring Extraction Tool - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Stuffing Box Lantern Ring Extraction Tool'. The
broader use case is: Field service hardware for removing lantern rings and packing remnants from
packed pump glands. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Segmented hook
extraction head All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Hooks or expands inside a gland cavity to pull worn sealing
components out from around a shaft. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Challenges CAD representation with slender curved hooks, expandable segmented geometry, shaft-
clearance voids, pin joints, and compact service-tool proportions. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 155 mm x 50 mm x 45 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=145 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 51 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=77 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 38 mm x 18 mm x 9 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 14 mm and undercut 2 mm for registration.

Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware
source.raw_idea_title: Rotating Equipment Shaft Alignment Target Bracket

requirements.part_name: Rotating Equipment Shaft Alignment Target Bracket - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Rotating Equipment Shaft Alignment Target Bracket'.
The broader use case is: Temporary bracket for mounting dial indicators or laser targets during pump
and motor alignment. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Clamp-on alignment
bracket body All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The
part must perform this mechanical role: Clamps onto a shaft or coupling hub and holds measurement
targets rigidly at adjustable offsets. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines split clamp geometry, hinge or fastener lugs, radial arms, accessory mounting
faces, and multiple datum-like surfaces that stress assembly-oriented modeling. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of
round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 79 mm and length 119 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 15 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 25 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 26 mm wide over 111 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=39 mm and X=79 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single cylindrical body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'axial through bore' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'end counterbores' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware
source.raw_idea_title: API Pump Seal Flush Manifold Block

requirements.part_name: API Pump Seal Flush Manifold Block - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'API Pump Seal Flush Manifold Block'. The broader
use case is: Compact service manifold for routing flush, quench, and drain lines around mechanical
seals. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Multi-port manifold block All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Routes process support fluids through labeled ports, valves, and instrument
connections near the seal gland. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Tests intersecting internal passage intent, external port bosses, plug pads, mounting ears, and face
labeling zones without needing actual fluid passage dimensions. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 180 mm x 55 mm x 45 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 6 mm on the length centerline at X=11 mm and X=169 mm, Y=27 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 60 mm long x 17 mm wide through the part, centered at X=90 mm, Y=27 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 45 mm x 18 mm x 13 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'API Pump Seal Flush Manifold Block'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware

requirements.part_name: Vertical Pump Lineshaft Bearing Spider - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Vertical Pump Lineshaft Bearing Spider'. The
broader use case is: Support component used inside vertical pump columns to center and stabilize a
rotating lineshaft. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Radial spider bearing
carrier All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Holds a central bearing carrier in position through radial arms
connected to the column wall. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Requires circular hub geometry, multiple radial arms, open flow windows, bearing-retainer features,
and symmetric relationships with service-specific interruptions. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar.
Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial
holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 71 mm and length 26 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 23 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 33 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 23 mm wide over 18 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M5 holes through the top flat at X=8 mm and X=17 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.

The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Vertical Pump Lineshaft Bearing Spider'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.domain: industrial pumps, seals, bearings, couplings, and rotating equipment service hardware

requirements.part_name: Coupling Hub Keyway Inspection Gauge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Coupling Hub Keyway Inspection Gauge'. The broader
use case is: Shop-floor gauge for checking wear and burrs around coupling hub bores and keyways. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Stepped bore-and-keyway gauge body All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Registers against hub bore and keyway surfaces to reveal service damage before
reassembly. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Exercises negative-
space representation, stepped cylindrical references, keyway-like protrusions, handle integration,
and inspection contact surfaces that are difficult to capture as simple solids. The part is made
from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one
rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets,
holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 120 mm x 85 mm x 70 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=17 mm and X=103 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 40 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=60 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 30 mm x 28 mm x 32 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 12 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 2 mm deep.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
